title: Center of Mass of a Rod with Quadratic Density
body: A rod of length L has a mass density that changes from one end to the other quadratically. The rod is laid out along the x-axis with one end at the origin and the other end at $$x=L$$, and the density is given by $$rho(x)=\rho_0+\left(\rho_1-\rho_0\right) \left(\frac{x}{L}\right)^2$$, where $$\rho_0$$ and $$\rho_1$$ are constant values.
